content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only .. 10. Разделение ответственности
Document lines: 277-328

## 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only

На записи у системы четыре обязательные задачи:

1. понять, стоит ли вообще писать память
2. найти похожие или конфликтующие items
3. превратить input в typed candidates
4. применить policy:
   - `insert`
   - `update`
   - `supersede`
   - `retract`
   - `noop`

Если пропустить retrieval/reconciliation, база быстро зарастает:
- дублями
- stale preferences
- conflicting notes
- task explosion
- summary spam

## 10. Разделение ответственности

### 10.1 User-facing runtime
Делает:
- диалог
- tool orchestration
- read-time retrieval
- cheap routing / escalation

Он не должен тащить на себе тяжелый reconciliation и repair.

### 10.2 Memory worker / manager
Делает:
- routing
- retrieval-before-update
- canonicalization
- note/claim extraction
- reconciliation
- consolidation
- repair
- retention

### 10.3 Storage layer
Отвечает за:
- immutable evidence
- typed tables
- transactional updates
- lineage
- indexes
- audit trail

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Update path is not append-only and requires retrieval/reconciliation summary=The architecture defines memory writes as an update workflow, not blind append: decide whether to write, retrieve similar/conflicting items, produce typed candidates, and apply policies (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes quality degradation such as duplicates, stale preferences, conflicts, task explosion, and summary spam. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 9. [1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Responsibility split across User-facing runtime, Memory worker / manager, and Storage layer summary=The design separates concerns: User-facing runtime handles dialog, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; Memory worker / manager performs heavier memory-update operations (routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, retention); Storage layer provides immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, ...[truncated 34 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<1>,entity:<2>,entity:<3> keywords=User-facing runtime,Memory worker / manager,Storage layer,separation of responsibilities tags=architecture,component_roles,doc_section_10 evidence=## 10. Разделение ответственности

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended pipeline routes routed writes through canonicalization, specialized updaters, reconciliation, and storage summary=In the recommended architecture, Memory Router outcomes (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed) feed Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized components (Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, Note Constructor) and reconcilers write to Postgres + pgvector, with background consolidation/repair/retention workers and runtime retrieval-answer loop. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory Router,Entity Canonicalizer,Postgres + pgvector,Read-Time Retrieval Planner tags=pipeline,recommended_architecture,component_roles useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`v3` memory architecture uses four axes to avoid brittle or dump-like memory summary=In `v3`, memory is organized along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope & lineage (scope_json/scope_text/source refs/origin note/memory runs). The document states all four are necessary to prevent transcri...[truncated 44 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=v3,four axes,representation lifecycle retrieval scope lineage tags=v3,architecture,memory_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture summary=Section 5 defines five storage tiers with distinct roles: Tier A for compact in-context prompt blocks, Tier B for typed long-term records, Tier C for semantic note-layer context, Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes across memory objects. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Storage tiers,Tier A,Tier B,Tier C,Tier D,Tier E tags=memory_architecture,doc_digest,storage_tiers useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
